front: Poem: ends with 'The wind is rising... we must try to live!'

front: Poem: discusses a 'worm unanswerable' — 'Love' and 'hatred of self' are equally valid names for this 'real-flesh eater'

front: Poem: 512 alexandrine couplets celebrating the French language
back: La Jeune Parque / The Young Fate (Paul Valéry)

front: Poem: broke the author's 'Great Silence' in 1917
back: La Jeune Parque / The Young Fate (Paul Valéry)

front: Poem: opens 'Who is that weeping, if not simply the wind?' with 'distant diamonds dying'
back: La Jeune Parque / The Young Fate (Paul Valéry)

front: Author: ~20-year 'Great Silence' hiatus from poetry began c. 1892 after a stormy night in Genoa
Author: 'Great Silence' provoked by the death of Stéphane Mallarmé
Author: during his silence, studied mathematics and law
Author: recorded thoughts in notebooks called Cahiers
Author: created intellectual character whose motto is 'Que peut un homme?' and who says 'stupidity is not my strong suit'
Author: aphorism 'a poem is never finished, only abandoned'
Author: Calvino quotes his aphorism 'One should be light like a bird, and not like a feather' discussing 'lightness'
Author: poem 'Palme' inspired James Merrill's 'Lost in Translation'
Poem: title creature declares 'Tremble, mortals — I'm strong when my insatiate yawn stretches to snap a spring'; Yvor Winters praised it as the greatest poem ever
Poem: serpent character calls the universe 'a defect in the purity of non-being'
Author: wrote a pseudo-Platonic dialogue where Eupalinos describes qualities of an architect
Author: filled Anatole France's seat on the Académie Française
Author: refused to write novels — Surrealist Manifesto cites his inability to write 'The Marquise went out at five o'clock'
Author: claimed 'all known literature is written in the language of common sense' except Rimbaud's
Author: advocated for 'pure poetry' that builds a 'system of reciprocal relations,' influenced by Poe's 'The Poetic Principle'
Author: Walter Benjamin opens 'The Work of Art in the Age of Mechanical Reproduction' with his quote about matter, space, and time
Author: Faust adaptation (Mon Faust) features secretary called Demoiselle Luste
Author: collaborated with Germaine Tailleferre on Cantate de Narcisse
Author: Miyazaki's film The Wind Rises takes its title from his poem
